optionList.content: "Well, a picture paints a thousand words."
"You just felt like destroying your textbooks?"
"Your parents and teachers are very concerned about your drawings."
"I am concerned about you. Are you now or have you ever been abused?"
"Well, a picture paints a thousand words."

"Your parents and teachers are very concerned about your drawings."

studentQuestionArray.content: A 10-year-old girl who has been referred for evaluation for drawing sexually explicit scenes in her textbooks says to the psychiatric nurse, "I just felt like it." Which response is therapeutic for the nurse to make in order to assess abuse-related symptoms?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale: 
The behaviors that this child engaged in are a warning signal of distress. The correct option is the only one that specifically addresses abuse. In option 1, the nurse is insensitive, sarcastic, and intrusive. In option 2, the nurse is assessing the client's destructive behaviors, not the possible sexual abuse history. In option 3, although the nurse is trying to assess the client's abuse-related symptoms, the nurse uses indirect means rather than straightforward expressions of the nurse's concern.

Client Needs: Psychosocial Integrity
Clinical Judgment/Cognitive Skill(s): Take Action
Cognitive Ability: Applying
Content Area: Mental Health
Health Problem: Mental Health: Abuse/Neglect
Integrated Process: Communication and Documentation
Priority Concepts: Interpersonal Violence, Sexuality
Strategies: Subject, Therapeutic Communication Techniques

Test-Taking Strategy:
Use knowledge of therapeutic communication techniques and focus on the subject, assessing for abuse-related symptoms; this will direct you to the correct option. Also note that the correct option is client-focused.

optionList.content: "Why didn't you just report your parents for this abuse?"
"What are you saying? Your parents abused you, so you got pregnant?"
"It seems that you needed your partner’s help to separate from your family."
"Sounds as if you decided to have a baby so you'd have someone for yourself."
"Why didn't you just report your parents for this abuse?"
"What are you saying? Your parents abused you, so you got pregnant?"
"It seems that you needed your partner’s help to separate from your family."
"Sounds as if you decided to have a baby so you'd have someone for yourself."

studentQuestionArray.content: A 15-year-old pregnant, unwed client tells the nurse, "My life was unbearable before I met my partner. One of my parents beats me every day, and my other parent has sexually abused me since I was 10 years old!" Which response is appropriate for the nurse to make?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationale: 
Adolescent pregnancy outside of marriage can arise from female low self-esteem, fears of inadequacy, and desperation to escape from an abusive and dysfunctional family. The most therapeutic communication technique is the one that uses restatement and repeats the main thought that the client expressed. This assures the client that the nurse is listening and is attempting to validate what the client has said. Options 1, 2, and 3 are nontherapeutic. Option 1 reflects a knowledge deficit on the nurse's part and places responsibility on the adolescent. Option 2 is insensitive and makes assumptions. Option 3 makes connections that are assumed and imply judgmental bias.

Client Needs: Psychosocial Integrity
Clinical Judgment/Cognitive Skill(s): Take Action
Cognitive Ability: Applying
Content Area: Mental Health
Health Problem: Mental Health: Abuse/Neglect
Integrated Process: Communication and Documentation
Priority Concepts: Stress and Coping, Interpersonal Violence
Strategies: Therapeutic Communication Techniques

Test-Taking Strategy:
Use knowledge of therapeutic communication techniques. The correct option is the only one that identifies a therapeutic technique, restatement.
